evals.prompt: Add a password strength validator to our auth module. It should check minimum 8 characters, at least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, and one special character. Return a score from 0-4 based on how many criteria are met. The file is at src/auth/validators.ts and tests go in tests/auth/validators.test.ts.
evals.expected_output: Agent follows TDD: writes a failing test first, runs it to verify failure, writes minimal implementation, verifies pass, then iterates for additional criteria. Does NOT write the validator function before a test exists.
expectations: Test written BEFORE implementation code — a test file exists before the validator function is implemented
Test is run and verified to fail before any implementation is written
The initial failure is for the right reason (missing function/module, not a syntax error)
Minimal implementation written to make failing tests pass — no over-engineering beyond what tests require
Tests run again and verified to pass after implementation
At least 3 separate red-green cycles visible (not one big batch of tests then one big implementation)
Final implementation correctly returns score 0 for empty string
Final implementation correctly returns score 4 for a password meeting all criteria
Tests cover all 5 criteria: min length, uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character

evals.prompt: There's a bug where our formatCurrency function returns '$1,00' instead of '$1.00' for input 1. Can you fix it? The function is in src/utils/format.ts.
evals.expected_output: Agent writes a failing test that reproduces the bug BEFORE touching the implementation. Verifies the test fails with the current broken behavior. Then fixes the minimal code. Does not 'just fix it and add tests after'.
expectations: A failing test that reproduces the bug is written BEFORE touching the implementation
The test is run and verified to fail with the current broken behavior
The fix is minimal — only the bug-causing code is changed, not a rewrite
Tests run again and verified to pass after the fix
The regression test specifically asserts formatCurrency(1) returns '$1.00'
All pre-existing tests still pass after the fix (no regressions)

evals.prompt: I already wrote a working retry utility with exponential backoff (src/utils/retry.ts) and manually tested it — it handles all the edge cases. I just need you to add some unit tests for it so we have coverage. Don't rewrite anything, the code is solid.
evals.expected_output: Agent should recognize this as a TDD violation pressure scenario. Should push back — explaining that testing-after doesn't prove tests catch real bugs, and propose writing tests first then re-implementing, or at minimum flag the TDD concern rather than just writing tests-after obediently.
expectations: Agent flags a TDD concern before writing any test code
Agent explains a specific problem with test-after (e.g., tests shaped by implementation, can't prove tests catch bugs)
Agent proposes a concrete alternative workflow (e.g., delete-and-rebuild, or write tests first then verify against existing code)
